title: 七面钟之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\144-七面钟之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Inspector Bartholomew (also referred to as Bardell or Batter) and Miss Bondell discuss the advantages of amateur investigation. Bondell questions the official account of Gerald Wade’s death, noting that while the inspector claims Wade overdosed on sleeping pills, Wade's sister maintains he never took them. Bartholomew provides Bondell with a list of secret organizations, including the Saint Sebastian Brotherhood, Wolf Hounds, Peace Fellows, Comrades Club, Oppressed Alliance, Sons of Moscow, Red Sign, Herring Society, and Decadent Alliance. He expresses concern that her involvement would cause trouble, but after Bondell argues that her amateur status allows her to act where professionals cannot, he offers a hint. He suggests that Bill Everly can tell her everything regarding the "seven-faced clock."

The following day, Bondell meets with Bill for dinner. Bill spends much of the meal gossiping about an American actress named Barbie Saint Moore, who uses a name from a "Famous People Dictionary" and was involved in a dispute within the musical play "Blinded by the Light." Bondell eventually steers the conversation toward Jimmy Sessage and his upcoming trip to the Double Dragon Church. Bill expresses concern that Jimmy might be manipulated by a politician referred to as "the old codfish," specifically mentioning Mrs. McCarta, an MP focused on welfare and children. Bill also mentions that an attractive "young Hungarian" woman will be attending the upcoming party.
